title: Minimum Cuts for Palindrome Partitioning
description:
Given Partition s into palindromic substrings such that the number of cuts is minimized. Return the minimum number of cuts required.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ |s| ≤ 2000
explanation:
Intuition
Precompute palindrome table pal[i][j]. Then dp[i] = min cuts to partition s[:i]. For each j<i, if pal[j][i-1] True, update dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[j]+1). dp[0]=-1 to offset final cut.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Expand palindromes centered or 2-D table fill
2. Main Algorithm: Compute dp over prefix lengths using pal table
3. Return Result: Answer is dp[n]

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^2)
- Space Complexity: O(n^2)

Key Insights
- Offset with dp[0]=-1 yields exact cut count
- Precompute pal reduces repeated checks

Common Pitfalls
- Skipping pal precomputation leads to O(n^3)
- Remember empty prefix base case

testCases.explanation: When min_cut_palindrome(s="aab"), the expected result is 1
